name: BMM_ENUMERATION
documentation: Definition of an enumeration type. In the BMM system, an 'enumeration' type is understood as an underlying basic type and a set of named constants of that type. It is designed so that the default type is Integer, and the default constants are numbered 0, 1, ... Optional model elements can be specified to override the values and / or the type.

item_names.name: item_names
item_names.documentation: The list of names of the enumeration. If no values are supplied, the integer values 0, 1, 2, ... are assumed.

item_values.name: item_values
item_values.documentation: Optional list of specific values. Must be 1:1 with `item_names' list.

underlying_type_name.name: underlying_type_name
underlying_type_name.documentation: Name of type any concrete BMM_ENUMERATION_* sub-type is based on, i.e. the name of type bound to 'T' in a declared use of this type.
